PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Jan-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Uccelli appeared by telephone for Robert Carter, Jr. Matthew Shafae for Linda Price.

Mr. Uccelli stated that there is a stipulation in the works to resolve issues involving the other Estate and Trust, that they have not had time to reduce it to writing and that the Parties elected to go with a Professional Fiduciary to handle all three matters. Robert Carter's estate case number is PES-13-296528. The Trust matter is not before the Court. The Court informed Counsel that it needs consents of Administrator, resignation, etc. The Court will have the Probate Examiner send Counsel notes as to how to proceed. Both Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary were continued to January 7, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to determine the issue of accountings (waived or required) and bond.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Jan-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Continued To Dec-03-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ralph Rizzo appearing specially for Attorney Matthew Shafae for and with Linda Price (Petitioner/Objector). David Uccelli for Robert Carter, Jr.

The Parties spoke of joint appointment of Linda and Robert, the $250,000.00 bank account that was closed out, the annuity that left Linda as the Beneficiary, the Decedent's medical condition, etc. The Court raised the issue of distrust amongst the Parties, the allegations, proposed an independent fiduciary to come in on this Estate, the Trust and the other Estate, and expressed concern about no bond. The Parties where asked to step out of the courtroom and talk. Upon returning, Counsel stated that they exchanged some names back and forth, and that they need to discuss the issue with their Clients. Both Petitions for Probate of Will were continued to December 3, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court asked Counsel to focus on the future on how to effectively administer this for their Clients and to focus on a solution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Not Reported)
